## Constants

<a name="Mat4Row0"></a>

```go
const (
    Mat4Row0 = iota
    Mat4Row1
    Mat4Row2
    Mat4Row3
)
```

<a name="Mat4Col0"></a>

```go
const (
    Mat4Col0 = iota
    Mat4Col1
    Mat4Col2
    Mat4Col3
)
```

<a name="Mat4x0y0"></a>

```go
const (
    Mat4x0y0 = x0y0
    Mat4x1y0 = x1y0
    Mat4x2y0 = x2y0
    Mat4x3y0 = x3y0
    Mat4x0y1 = x0y1
    Mat4x1y1 = x1y1
    Mat4x2y1 = x2y1
    Mat4x3y1 = x3y1
    Mat4x0y2 = x0y2
    Mat4x1y2 = x1y2
    Mat4x2y2 = x2y2
    Mat4x3y2 = x3y2
    Mat4x0y3 = x0y3
    Mat4x1y3 = x1y3
    Mat4x2y3 = x2y3
    Mat4x3y3 = x3y3
)
```

<a name="TransformWorkGroup"></a>

```go
const (
    TransformWorkGroup      = "transform"
    TransformResetWorkGroup = "transformReset"
)
```

<a name="DegToRadVal"></a>

```go
const DegToRadVal = (math.Pi / 180.0)
```

<a name="FloatMax"></a>

```go
const FloatMax = Float(math.MaxFloat32)
```

<a name="FloatSmallestNonzero"></a>

```go
const FloatSmallestNonzero = Float(math.SmallestNonzeroFloat32)
```

<a name="RadToDegVal"></a>

```go
const RadToDegVal = (180.0 / math.Pi)
```

<a name="Roughly"></a>

```go
const Roughly = 0.001
```

<a name="Tiny"></a>

```go
const Tiny = 0.0001
```

<a name="Qw"></a>

```go

    Qw  QuaternionComponent = iota
    Qx
    Qy
    Qz
```

<a name="Transform.ScaleWithoutChildren"></a>
### func \(\*Transform\) [ScaleWithoutChildren](<https://github.com/JrSchmidtt/kaiju/blob/master/src/matrix/transform.go#L404>)

```go
func (t *Transform) ScaleWithoutChildren(scale Vec3)
```

ScaleWithoutChildren will scale a transform without changing the world scale of the children of this transform. That is to say, it will update all the child transform scales to return to their original world scale after scaling this transform.

<a name="Vec3.SignedAngle"></a>
### func \(Vec3\) [SignedAngle](<https://github.com/JrSchmidtt/kaiju/blob/master/src/matrix/vec3.go#L317>)

```go
func (v Vec3) SignedAngle(other Vec3, axis Vec3) Float
```

SignedAngle returns the signed angle \(in radians\) from v to other around the given axis. The sign is positive for counterclockwise rotation when looking along the axis direction. Assumes non\-zero vectors; returns 0 if v or other is zero\-length or they are equal. For best precision, normalize v, other, and axis before calling if they aren't already.
